title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
this refers to the object that is currently executing the function. Its value is determined by HOW the function is called, not where it is defined (except for arrow functions).

Binding rules (in priority order):
1. new binding — using new, this = the new object being created.
2. Explicit binding — using .call(obj), .apply(obj), or .bind(obj), this = obj.
3. Implicit binding — called as a method: obj.fn(), this = obj.
4. Default binding — standalone call: fn(), this = global (or undefined in strict mode).
5. Arrow functions — no own this; inherit from enclosing lexical scope.

Common mistake: extracting a method from an object loses its this binding.
